heading: Same-Sex Spousal Sponsorship in Canada
description: As a permanent resident or Canadian citizen, you have the opportunity to bring your same-sex partner to Canada through the permanent residence application process. Canada, known for its progressive values, respects privacy and individual liberty for all residents. In 2005, the country legally recognized same-sex marriages, leading to the inclusion of common-law partners, same-sex spouses, and conjugal partners in the Citizenship and Immigration Canada (CIC) policies.
eligibileSubHead: If you’re in a same-sex relationship and your partner resides outside Canada, you can sponsor them to join you in Canada. To qualify, consider the following criteria:
e1: Permanent Resident or Canadian Citizen: You must be either a permanent resident or a Canadian citizen.
e2: Marriage in Canada: If you and your same-sex partner are married within Canada, and possess a valid marriage certificate issued by a Canadian province or territory, you meet the eligibility requirements.
e3: Marriage Outside Canada: If your marriage occurred outside Canada, you can still apply for spousal sponsorship or conjugal partner sponsorship if the marriage is legally recognized in Canada and complies with the laws of the country where it took place.
SameSexPartSponPara: In Canada, two partner categories are acknowledged: conjugal and common-law. Even if you’re not legally married to your same-sex partner, you can sponsor them to reside in Canada together.
WhatCommonLawPartHeading: What is a Common Law Partner?
WhatCommonLawPartPara: A Common Law Partner refers to an individual with whom you share a continuous living arrangement for more than one year, maintaining a conjugal relationship. Whether your partner is of the opposite or same gender, this definition applies for immigration purposes. Demonstrating a significant level of commitment between both parties is essential.
ProvYourCommonLawPartDesc: To establish your common-law relationship, consider the following aspects:
py1: Shared Residence: You and your partner must reside in the same home, sharing responsibilities such as household chores.Establishment in B.C.
py2: Financial and Emotional Support: Provide evidence of mutual financial arrangements and emotional support.
py3: Parenting Together: If applicable, demonstrate that you have children together.
py4: Public Presentation: Present yourselves as a couple in public settings.
py5: Personal Behavior: Factors like fidelity, commitment, and feelings toward each other contribute to the proof.
CommonLawRequDocuSponHeading: Common Law: Required Documents for Sponsorship
CommonLawRequDocuSponPara: When applying, submit at least two of the following documents to validate your common-law relationship:
crd1: Pay stubs or tax forms showing the same address.
crd2: Proof of living together for a minimum of one year.
crd3: Evidence of property ownership or shared rent
crd8: Birth certificates or adoption records for shared children.
crd10: Affirmation from family and friends (letters, emails, social media)
CommonLawRequDocuSponPara2: If specific documents are unavailable, explore alternative ways to demonstrate your relationship. Sworn declarations or letters from trusted sources can strengthen your case. Ultimately, the visa officer’s discretion will determine the acceptance of your common-law documents, so providing ample proof enhances your chances of approval.
WhoQualConjHeading: Who Qualifies as a Conjugal Partner?
WhoQualConjDisc: A Conjugal Partner refers to an individual living outside of Canada with whom you share a committed and romantic relationship lasting at least one year. However, significant barriers prevent you from residing together. These barriers may include factors beyond their control, such as immigration restrictions, religious considerations, or sexual orientation.
Q1: Barriers to Living Together: Despite the inability to cohabit, your relationship must demonstrate commitment and genuine connection.
Q2: Legal Marriage Constraints: Some conjugal partners cannot legally marry their sponsor but still qualify under this category.
Q3: Similarities to Common-Law and Spousal Relationships: In all other aspects, conjugal partnerships mirror common-law unions or marriages, requiring a bona fide relationship lasting at least one year.
c1: Country-Specific Challenges: If your relationship faces obstacles due to factors like a country’s stance on same-sex marriage, lack of divorce options, or legal restrictions, it still qualifies as a conjugal relationship.
c2: Emotional, Financial, and Physical Interdependence: Conjugal partners must rely on each other emotionally, financially, and physically.
ConjPartReqDocSponHeading: Common Law: Required Documents for Sponsorship
ConjPartReqDocSponPara: When applying, submit at least two of the following documents to validate your common-law relationship:
cprd5: Police certificates from countries where your spouse lived for six months or longer after turning 18.
cprd7: Proof of payment for government fees
cprd9: Relationship information and sponsorship evaluation questionnaire
cprd11: Birth certificates or adoption records for shared children.
cprd12: Proof of marriage registration with a government authority

q1: Can I bring my same-sex partner to Canada?
qa1: Yes, you have the option to accompany your same-sex partner to Canada as a permanent resident. Canada stands out as one of the most forward-thinking nations globally, prioritizing privacy and freedom for all its inhabitants. Legal recognition of same-sex marriages was granted nationwide in 2005.
q2: How long are you responsible for sponsoring a spouse, common-law partner, or conjugal partner in Canada?
qa2: The duration of the undertaking for sponsorship varies depending on the province, with a standard period of 3 years for sponsoring a spouse, common-law partner, or conjugal partner in all provinces except Quebec.
q3: What are the obligations of a same-sex sponsor in Canada?
qa3: Your responsibilities as a sponsor entail signing an undertaking, and committing to providing financial assistance for the essential requirements of your spouse or partner and any dependent children they may have.
q4: Can my same-sex spouse stay in Canada while waiting for sponsorship?
qa4: Yes, provided they maintain their legal status. Temporary resident status is granted for a designated duration, and it's imperative to ensure its ongoing validity while residing in Canada.
